content.text: Song Mode (added in OS 2.0) allows you to chain scenes together into a complete song arrangement. With up to 99 song positions and patterns up to 99 bars long, you can create compositions up to 9,801 bars in length.
Song Mode was one of the most requested features and was added in the OS 2.0 'Champions' update.
To enter Song Mode, hold SHIFT and press TEMPO/LOOP. The display will show 'SONG' and the current song position. You can now arrange scenes into a complete song.
A song is made up of song positions. Each position specifies:

items: How many times the scene repeats

content.text: In Song Mode, use the number keys to select a scene for the current position. Use +/- to navigate between song positions. The song plays through each position in order.

items: Navigate to the position you want to edit with +/-
Select a scene using number keys
Set the repeat count if desired
Move to the next position and repeat

content.text: Press PLAY in Song Mode to play the song from the current position. The EP-133 will progress through each position automatically. You can jump to any position during playback.
A typical song arrangement might look like:
By default, patterns loop continuously (Loop Mode). Song Mode changes this behavior so the EP-133 progresses through your arranged song positions instead of looping a single scene.

content.title: Live Performance
content.text: You can still manually trigger scene changes during Song Mode playback for live improvisation.
With 99 song positions and patterns up to 99 bars each, the theoretical maximum song length is 9,801 bars. At 120 BPM in 4/4 time, that's over 5 hours of music!
